content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To     : Barbara Millman
        Fair Political Practices
        Commission

From   : Governor's Office

FPPC

MAR 0 9 57 AM '84    Date  :  March 5, 1984

Subject:

     This memorandum confirms my separate conversations with
you and Bob Leidigh of your office regarding the reporting
of limousine services provided to the Governor during state
business trips. It is my understanding that such services,
unless reimbursed by the state, are reportable as gifts if
the value exceeds $50 per year from a single donor. In
determining value, the Governor at his option may utilize the
local commercial taxicab rate or the charter limousine rate
divided by the number of passengers. Reimbursement of the
service provider at either of the foregoing rates within 30
days of the travel or within the ordinary billing cycle of
the service provider will be deemed to be full consideration
for the services provided and will relieve the Governor of the
reporting obligation.

     I trust that this memorandum accurately summarizes our
conversations. Please contact me if it is inaccurate in
any respect.

     Thank you for your prompt and courteous responses to
my primary questions on the Political Reform Act.


                                    Vance Raye
                                    Vance W. Raye
                                    Legal Affairs Secretary

sections.question_synthetic: How should limousine services provided to the Governor during state business trips be reported under the Political Reform Act, and what determines whether such services constitute reportable gifts?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming memorandum from the Governor's Office to FPPC staff confirming prior conversations, not an FPPC formal advice letter response. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present. The document appears to be a confirmation of informal FPPC guidance already provided verbally. Confidence is moderate due to lack of standard advice letter structure.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: How should limousine services provided to the Governor during state business trips be reported under the Political Reform Act, and what determines whether such services constitute reportable gifts?

It is my understanding that such services, unless reimbursed by the state, are reportable as gifts if the value exceeds $50 per year from a single donor. In determining value, the Governor at his option may utilize the local commercial taxicab rate or the charter limousine rate divided by the number of passengers. Reimbursement of the service provider at either of the foregoing rates within 30 days of the travel or within the ordinary billing cycle of the service provider will be deemed to be full consideration for the services provided and will relieve the Governor of the reporting obligation. I trust that this memorandum accurately summarizes our conversations. Please contact me if it is inaccurate in any respect. Thank you for your prompt and courteous responses to my primary questions on the Political Reform Act. Vance Raye Vance W. Raye Legal Affairs Secretary
embedding.summary: This is a memorandum from the Governor's Office to FPPC staff confirming prior conversations regarding the reporting requirements for limousine services provided to the Governor during state business trips, including valuation methods and reimbursement procedures that would eliminate reporting obligations.
